scope: Compact agent-usability gap report for current data files after API examples, LibMoonCat surfaces, color-hue improvements, task recipes, and routing updates.

method.summary: Reviewed requested files, status fields, TODO arrays, limitations, direct data/agent-index.json routing coverage, and current data/*.json inventory.
limitations: This file does not add MoonCat facts or verify upstream freshness.
This file does not modify sourceRefs, import datasets, or resolve listed gaps.
Routing coverage is checked against direct data/agent-index.json references in primaryFiles and optionalFiles.

notes: data/task-recipes.json is now directly routed by data/agent-index.json.
data/kb-gap-index.json is indirectly mentioned by data/task-recipes.json, but it is not directly routed from data/agent-index.json.
No routing changes were made in this pass because data/agent-index.json was out of scope.

resolvedOrPartiallyResolvedGaps.notes: Agents now have compact checked and manifest-derived API examples; examples still do not replace endpoint manifests or OpenAPI schemas.
Observed helper names and conservative input/output surfaces are indexed without importing source code or full mappings.
Previous empty hue/special/pale structures are replaced by aggregate observed snapshot data and guardrails; canonical rendering, per-cat mappings, and exact palettes remain unresolved.
Common agent workflows now have load order, outputs, guardrails, and stop conditions, and the recipe file is routed from data/agent-index.json.
Rescue bucket status now records method/timestamp verification; identifier conversion and downstream-use boundaries still need caution.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as a compact endpoint manifest, but agents should not treat it as full OpenAPI schema or verified runtime behavior for every response edge case.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Re-sync from registered OpenAPI sources or live-check selected endpoints only in a focused API pass.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for constructing common requests, but some examples are placeholders and examples do not replace the endpoint manifest or OpenAPI specs.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Add more checked examples only when a concrete workflow needs them, without storing response bodies.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for exact community-curated membership arrays, but agents need the index convention before downstream UI/tool use.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Add or adjust memberships only from sourced community data and preserve the rescue-order-index boundary.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for category definitions and notes, but incomplete for exact memberships and premium counts.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Keep definitions separate from membership arrays and add missing counts only from sourced data.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Much more useful for aggregate observed hue/color context, but not a canonical rendering, exact palette, or per-cat color lookup source.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Review parser/rendering or canonical color sources separately before adding exact palettes, formulas, or per-cat mappings.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for choosing contract/source surfaces, but not a call-signature, ABI, source, bytecode, or internals index.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: If exact call signatures are needed, create a separate ABI policy or artifact index instead of expanding this routing file.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for verified address/source identity records, but not for detailed materialization internals or imported artifact blobs.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Review materialization internals and accessory scope in focused passes before importing derived behavior or artifact references.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for current identifier warnings and verified lookup boundaries, but older wrapper conventions and some derivation boundaries remain unresolved.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Separately verify older wrapper token ID conventions and keep bytes5 conversion described as array-backed lookup, not formula.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Not yet useful for CID lookup beyond indicating that CID data is unverified.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Verify CIDs from primary project, metadata, or repository sources and record what each CID contains.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as an audit report, but agents relying only on data/agent-index.json will not discover it directly.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Add a direct data/agent-index.json route to this gap index in a routing-focused pass if desired.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for helper discovery and conservative input/output routing, but not full source-code documentation or canonical behavior proof.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Review specific helper behavior only when a task needs implementation details beyond the surface index.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as preserved navigation/research links, but not a canonical fact source by itself.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Promote any link to sources.json only in a source-focused pass with trust and limitation notes.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for top-level type scaffolding, but agents must keep community-curated categories separate from official protocol traits.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Clarify derivation methods and identifier conversions before using bucket/type values outside their source context.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for project navigation, but not enough for canonical data values or trust-sensitive claims.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Add ownership and trust-level notes for each link while keeping links separate from canonical facts.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for source-derived constants, but not enough to infer rescue ranges, category membership, or parser behavior beyond recorded constants.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Verify parser source and MD5 workflow before deriving image-generation or trait data.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for existing bucket arrays with improved method context, but values still need identifier-boundary checks before API, token, or bytes5 use.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Use data/identifier-conventions.json and task recipes before applying bucket values outside rescue-order-index contexts.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as range and method scaffolding, but early-rescue meanings and some operational boundaries remain underspecified.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Define early-rescue ranges by rescue order, block, timestamp, or community convention before using them operationally.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Core source index is usable, but source/method entries for derived data and future exact values remain incomplete.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Add derived data entries only after documenting the generating source or method.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful as special-category scaffold, but exact memberships and protocol/community boundaries are not fully materialized.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Separate canonical protocol categories from community-curated categories and add exact membership only after source verification.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for sequencing common tasks, but recipes can drift if underlying data files change without a workflow refresh.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Refresh recipes after future curated trait, image, color, API, or source imports change accepted workflows.

fileGaps.agentUsabilityImpact: Useful for trait field schema and validation metadata, but not for per-cat trait lookup, full frequencies, names, or accessory details.
fileGaps.nextDataPass: Define a curated trait data model before promoting normalized per-cat fields into data/.

notableNonGaps.note: Routes all current data/*.json files except itself and data/kb-gap-index.json.
Fills the previous agent need for small API construction examples while preserving example limitations.
Fills the previous helper-surface discoverability gap without importing source code.
Provides workflow sequencing and guardrails for common agent tasks.

recommendedNextPasses.reason: data/ipfs-cids.json is still needs-verification with an empty cids array.
data/kb-gap-index.json is the only current data file, besides data/agent-index.json itself, not directly routed from data/agent-index.json.
Identifier guidance is improved, but older wrapper token ID conventions and some cross-surface boundaries remain unresolved.
data/color-hues.json now has aggregate observed hue data, but canonical palettes, exact rendering behavior, and per-cat mappings remain out of scope.
data/trait-index.json is schema metadata only; a separate model decision is needed before any curated per-cat trait import.
data/sources.json remains incomplete for some future exact values and derived-data methods.
data/project-links.json is useful navigation but still lacks complete ownership and trust notes.
